「直到……」，等於until，後面接某個時間或階段。up to亦表「最多有……」，後面接數值或程度。

grammar.formula: so as to VR表「為了……；以便……」，
grammar.explanation: 用於表示目的，否定用法為so as not to VR。so as to和in order to意思相同，但in order to可置於句首，so as to不行。

3.title: Lesson 3 Progress1 and Preservation2: Striking a Balance

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 進步；進展　the process of improving or getting closer to [`prɑgrɛs]a goal

vocabulary.example_en: Work on the project is progressing steadily, so we expect it to be finished on time.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 維護；保存　the act of keeping something in the same or [ˏprɛzɚ`veʃən]good condition

vocabulary.example_en: While we have updated most parts of the house, we have made sure to preserve its original character.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 短暫的體驗、感受　a short experience of something that [glɪmps]helps you understand it

vocabulary.example_en: Since Ronnie only caught a glimpse of the woman in the elevator, he couldn’t tell whether she was the new manager or not.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 認同　the qualities and attitudes that make a person or [aɪ`dɛntətɪ]people different from others

vocabulary.example_en: The police are still working to determine the identity of the man who tried to break into the house.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 發展　the process of growing bigger or better or [dɪ`vɛləpmənt]becoming more advanced
usually pl. 瑰寶(尤指歷史或藝術珍品)　something very [`trɛʒɚ]valuable or special, especially a piece of art or a historical object

vocabulary.example_en: If we keep digging in the sand, maybe we will find some buried treasure.
Eric really treasures this old watch because it was a gift from his grandfather.

vocabulary.chinese: 取代；代替　to take the place of something or be used [rɪ`ples]instead of something

vocabulary.example_en: The microwave Sally just bought was broken, so she took it back to the store to get a replacement.

vocabulary.chinese: 危及；危害　to cause someone or something to be in danger
vocabulary.example_en: The nearby factory has been polluting the river, which endangers the lives of creatures living in or near the water.

vocabulary.chinese: sing., [U] 大量；豐盛　a very large quantity; the state of [ə`bʌndəns]having more than enough of something

vocabulary.example_en: The country is lucky to have an abundant water supply, thanks to its many lakes.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] (尤指政治、商業或軍事的)活動；運動　a series of [kæm`pen]activities meant to achieve a particular social, commercial, or political goal

vocabulary.example_en: The group is campaigning for a change to the labor laws to reduce the maximum working hours.

vocabulary.chinese: 作出結論；斷定　to make a decision about something based [kən`klud]on what you have heard or seen

vocabulary.example_en: After thinking over all the facts for a long time, the detective came to the conclusion that the death really was an accident.

vocabulary.chinese: (用交通工具)運輸；輸送　to use a vehicle to move [træns`port]something or somebody from one place to another

vocabulary.example_en: My car is my main mode of transportation, but I also take the bus occasionally.

vocabulary.chinese: 位於；坐落在　located in a certain place or position
vocabulary.example_en: This apartment is situated on the town’s main road, so it is not very quiet.
It is best to situate the tree in an area that receives enough sunlight throughout the day.

vocabulary.chinese: 淹沒　to cover something in water or with a liquid
vocabulary.example_en: The small village was drowned by the heavy flood, causing dozens of deaths and hundreds of injuries.
A two-year-old boy almost drowned after falling into a lake, but thankfully his father saved him in time.

vocabulary.chinese: 使避免　to save somebody from having to experience [spɛr]something unpleasant

vocabulary.example_en: I know you’re very busy this morning, but can you spare a few minutes? I have something important to discuss with you.

vocabulary.chinese: 極珍貴的；極重要的　so valuable or important that the [`praɪslɪs]price cannot be calculated
緩解；減輕　to make something bad or painful become less [rɪ`liv]serious

vocabulary.example_en: It was a huge relief to hear that my grandmother was going to make a full recovery.

vocabulary.chinese: 城市的；都市的　related to a town or city
vocabulary.example_en: Urban renewal projects have improved the city’s appearance and provided people with a better living environment.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 居民；住戶　a person who lives or has their home in a [`rɛzədənt]certain place

vocabulary.example_en: Buckingham Palace has served as the official residence of Britain’s head of state since 1837.

vocabulary.chinese: 至關重要的；決定性的　extremely important
vocabulary.example_en: Teachers play a crucial role in the education and lives of the students that they teach.

vocabulary.chinese: 緊急的；緊迫的　needing to be dealt with immediately
vocabulary.example_en: This man is in urgent need of medical attention and needs to get to the hospital quickly.
Fixing the bridge is a matter of urgency, as people cannot leave or enter the town without it.

vocabulary.chinese: 極大的；龐大的　extremely large in size or amount
vocabulary.example_en: The actor owns an enormous three-story mansion with a home theater and a rooftop pool.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 任務　an official job assigned to a person or group of [`mɪʃən]people and often requiring travel
快樂美好的；深情的　[when before a noun] kind, happy, [fɑnd]or loving

vocabulary.example_en: My roommate and I didn’t get along at first, but I’ve grown fond of him over these years.

vocabulary.chinese: 埃及（人）的　n. [C] 埃及人

grammar.explanation: 「因此」，用來表示結果。同 as a result, consequently, hence, thus
「然而」，用來表達語氣的轉折。
「因此；結果」，用來表示後果。同 consequently, hence, therefore, thus

grammar.formula: as a result of sth.則表
grammar.explanation: 「由於……」，用來表示原因。

grammar.formula: It is/was concluded that + S + V表
grammar.explanation: 「結論是……」，it是虛主詞，用來代替that the temples...to higher ground。
「某人免於某事」，為spare sb. sth.的被動說法。

grammar.formula: after all為轉承語，表「畢竟」，用來說明前文的理由，可置於句首、句中或句尾。after all另一個
grammar.explanation: 意思是「終究」，表示在各種考量或情況下最終的結果，通常置於句尾。

vocabulary.chinese: 好冒險的　willing to do new things or travel to new places, even if it is a bit risky or dangerous
vocabulary.example_en: Rita is a very adventurous person, so it doesn’t surprise me that she wants to ride a motorcycle around Taiwan.
In the movie, the three friends go on a wonderful adventure that takes them beyond the magic forest.

vocabulary.chinese: 當地的；本地的　originating and living naturally in a particular place, rather than being introduced from somewhere else
vocabulary.example_en: Many indigenous languages are at risk of disappearing as younger people generally speak national languages.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 部落　a group of people who share the same culture and live together in a specific area
vocabulary.example_en: There are more than five hundred different Native American tribes in the US. Each of these groups has its own language and history.
During our visit to the mountain village, the chief told us all about the tribal customs and traditions of his people.

vocabulary.chinese: 持續；貫穿　(spanned—spanned—spanning) to cover a certain period of time; to continue throughout a specific time period
vocabulary.example_en: Whereas World War I lasted only four years, World War II spanned a period of six years.
Although only five, Laura has a very long attention span and can easily listen to a story for one hour.

vocabulary.chinese: 多種多樣的；多個的　 involving many different things or people; very many
vocabulary.example_en: Jason was rushed to a nearby hospital with multiple injuries from the car crash.

vocabulary.chinese: vt. 成倍增加；迅速增加
vocabulary.example_en: Patrick’s problems started to multiply after he lost his job.

vocabulary.chinese: 繁榮的；富裕的　having success and lots of money
vocabulary.example_en: After gold was discovered in the area, many locals became very prosperous and could buy whatever they liked.
When Lewis’s daughter started her own business, he gave her a red envelope and wished her luck and prosperity.
Since the war ended, the country has produced more goods and has prospered as a result.

vocabulary.chinese: 豐富的；大量的　being so much or so many that there’s no need to worry about running out of it
vocabulary.example_en: Every time members of the tribe cross the desert on camels, they take a plentiful supply of water with them.
Since most hotels offer food in plenty at their breakfast buffets, no guest is likely to go hungry.

vocabulary.chinese: 精巧的；精心製作的　made with great care and patience; having lots of careful details
vocabulary.example_en: As Christmas approaches, large stores often put up elaborate displays of decorations and lights to attract more customers.

vocabulary.chinese: vt. 詳盡闡述；詳盡描述
vocabulary.example_en: The manager first gave the main reason for the meeting, and then started elaborating on all the details.

vocabulary.chinese: 請求；懇求；呼籲　to ask for something, such as help or support, in a very sincere way
vocabulary.example_en: The police appealed to anyone with more information about the crime to come forward and help them catch the killer.
At the end of her speech, the mayor made an appeal to all citizens to do their part to protect the environment.
Arthur is quiet and shy, so a loud party with lots of strangers holds very little appeal for him.

vocabulary.chinese: 淺的　not having much distance between the top or surface and the bottom
vocabulary.example_en: This pool is very shallow. Even at its deepest point, the water only comes up to one’s knees.
It was pretty shallow of Tammy to dump her boyfriend just because she didn’t like his new hairstyle.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 物種　(pl. species) a specific group of plants or animals whose members are similar and can breed with each other
vocabulary.example_en: Though leopards and tigers both belong to the cat family, they’re completely different species and unlikely to mate successfully.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 融洽；和睦　a state when people or other creatures can happily live, work, or exist together in peace
vocabulary.example_en: Despite their differences, the two tribes learned to live in harmony with each other and share the area’s plentiful resources.

vocabulary.chinese: [C, U] 收穫；收成　the time when natural resources, like crops or fish, are ready to be collected by farmers or fishers
vocabulary.example_en: California’s peach harvests in May are followed shortly after by the apple harvests in June.

vocabulary.chinese: vi. 收割（莊稼）；捕獵（動物、魚）
vocabulary.example_en: It’s important to harvest all the crops before winter comes. Otherwise, they’ll just die and be wasted.

vocabulary.chinese: 保證；擔保　to make sure that what is expected, promised, or asked for will happen
vocabulary.example_en: To guarantee a safe trip, don’t drink before driving and stick to the speed limit.
Though it sounds like a good idea, there’s no guarantee it’ll work. All we can do is try it and see.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] usually pl. 指導方針；準則　rules or tips for doing something in the right way
vocabulary.example_en: None of her students had done a science project before, so Ms. Dobbs drew up some guidelines for them to follow.

vocabulary.chinese: 私有的；私用的　belonging to a specific owner or user; for personal rather than public use
vocabulary.example_en: Using a small private jet for four passengers costs around US$5,000 per hour, so it is more expensive than flying first class.
When the patient and his wife began discussing family matters, Dr. Bates left the room to give them some privacy.

vocabulary.chinese: 試吃；品嚐 to try or taste a kind of new food by just having a very small amount
vocabulary.example_en: Would you like to sample some of our freshly baked cupcakes? We also have cookies you can try. Both are on sale today.
To truly sample the life of peace and quiet that monks lead, temple guests must keep their cellphones turned off throughout their stay.
During the meeting, Helen handed out samples of the cream so everyone could try the new product.

vocabulary.chinese: 破壞　to make something turn bad or fail, usually by doing something that one is not supposed to do
vocabulary.example_en: “Of course I won’t tell Tracy about the party. After all, I don’t want to spoil the surprise.”

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 結果；後果　an effect or result of something that has happened
vocabulary.example_en: The consequence of Eddie’s poor attitude toward his schoolwork was that he failed to get into a good university.

vocabulary.chinese: 隨之發生的；作為結果的
vocabulary.example_en: Though the typhoon itself wasn’t a particularly powerful one, the consequent flooding caused a lot of damage.

vocabulary.chinese: 潛水　to swim underwater with masks, air tanks, etc. to help someone breathe
vocabulary.example_en: Three divers will dive along the ocean floor and spend forty-five minutes exploring the remains of the old ship.

vocabulary.chinese: 干擾；打擾　to prevent something from happening in the normal or usual way; to interrupt someone when he or she is doing something
vocabulary.example_en: Don’t talk too loudly inside the church; otherwise, you’ll disturb those two people saying their prayers.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 打擾；干擾；引起混亂或騷動的事物
vocabulary.example_en: Workers on the roof made so much noise that the neighbors told the police about the disturbance.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 智慧；才智　the ability to make sensible judgments, usually gained through lots of life experience
vocabulary.example_en: The old chief was known for his wisdom and helped many of the tribe’s members make key life decisions.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] （某地的）居民、棲息動物　a person or an animal that lives in a specific area, place, country, etc.
vocabulary.example_en: Upon learning that some factories had been polluting the local water supply, many inhabitants moved out of the area.
Though we’re not ready to live there yet, scientists believe that humans will one day be able to inhabit the planet Mars.

grammar.explanation: 「被稱為……」，known常搭配的介系詞比較如下：
為「地球之肺」，因為它為全世界製造大量的氧氣。

grammar.formula: be at the heart of B指
grammar.explanation: 「A在B的中心」，此處偏重抽象意涵，強調A在B當中的重要性。
「附帶狀況」，表「有……」、「伴隨著……」。若受詞和後面的動詞關係是主動，用現在分詞作為受詞補語；若受詞和後面的動詞關係是被動，則用過去分詞；若受詞後面是形容詞／介系詞（片語）則表示「狀態」。
「被限制於……」。此處to是介系詞，後接「動名詞」時，表示主詞被限制只能做某些動作；亦可接名詞（場所），則表示活動範圍被限制在某處。

grammar.formula: at the end of為介系詞片語，表
grammar.explanation: 「在……的結束、尾端」。in the end則為轉承語，表「最後；終於」。
「以……作結束」。conclude亦可作及物動詞，conclude A with B指「以B結束A」。

grammar.formula: no longer表「不再」，可放在be動詞或助動詞之後，一般動詞之前，亦可放在句尾，
grammar.explanation: 意為「過去會做某事，但現在停止了」。同 not...anymore, not...any longer
「因此」，用來表示結果。同 as a result, hence, thus

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 特務　a special government worker or spy who goes on secret missions or who gets secret information about other countries’ governments
vocabulary.example_en: As a secret agent, James Bond often has to spy on foreign government officials without getting caught.

vocabulary.chinese: [C]  (企業、政治等的) 代理人；經紀人
vocabulary.example_en: Since our travel agent had arranged all the flights and hotels for us, we didn’t have to make any bookings ourselves.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 服務機構；(尤指) 代理機構
vocabulary.example_en: Large companies often rely on employment agencies to help them find the right person for the job.

vocabulary.chinese: 猛衝；突然出現　(burst-burst-bursting) to go in or come out very suddenly, usually in a way that gets others’ attention or surprises them
vocabulary.example_en: When Charlie heard a loud scream coming from the kitchen, he burst into the room to see if everyone was okay.
When hearing the news of her son’s engagement, Mrs. Tomson burst into tears of joy.
We saw flames and smoke and then, with a sudden burst of energy, the rocket shot up into the air.

vocabulary.chinese: 逮捕　to catch and keep someone who has broken the law
vocabulary.example_en: The singer was arrested for drunk driving and questioned at the police station for several hours.
After being chased for five days, the 31-year-old man is finally under arrest and will be charged with several crimes.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 嘆氣；嘆息  the sound one makes to show that he or she is tired, relieved, disappointed, etc.
vocabulary.example_en: Josh, not wanting to argue in the store, just let out a sigh and quietly allowed his mother to choose the curtains.
There was nothing Jane could say or do to correct her mistake. All she could do was sigh with regret.

vocabulary.chinese: 有同情心的；表示憐憫的 feeling sorry for and wanting to help those who are in pain or in need
vocabulary.example_en: The boss is such a compassionate person that her employees can take two weeks for paid leave if they lose a family member.
Out of compassion for the poor old beggar, Sandy bought him a lunchbox and some warm soup.

vocabulary.chinese: 奪取；攻占　to take or control something, usually suddenly and with a lot of force
vocabulary.example_en: After months of fighting, the armed rebels overthrew the government and successfully seized power.
During the arrest, Captain Andrews managed to seize the weapon from the suspect before she could shoot anybody.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 飢餓；餓死　suffering or death due to a lack of food
vocabulary.example_en: With very little to eat on the lifeboat, the three survivors would have faced certain starvation if they hadn’t been rescued quickly.

vocabulary.chinese: （軍隊、警察等）封鎖；關閉　to use force to tightly close or block the way so that someone or something can’t enter or get out
vocabulary.example_en: To stop resistance leaders from escaping abroad, the local army sealed all the borders between their state and the neighboring states.
Once the jar is filled with jam, seal it very tightly so no air, insects, or moisture can get in.
King Henry finished his letter to his daughter, closed the envelope, and stamped it with his royal seal.

vocabulary.chinese: 動員（某人）以幫助　to find someone willing to do something for you
vocabulary.example_en: During the Olympics, local students were recruited to help with all the preparations needed for the different events.
With so many Chinese tourists visiting the restaurant, the owner has started recruiting Mandarin speakers to join her staff.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 同事；同僚　someone who works at the same company, store, organization, or school as you; a co-worker
vocabulary.example_en: Virginia shares an office with six colleagues. They all get along well and often have dinner together right after work.

vocabulary.chinese: 偷帶；偷拿　to take or bring someone or something into or out of a place without permission
vocabulary.example_en: Lily was caught trying to sneak snacks into the movie theater and was asked to leave them at the front desk.
While Harry’s back was turned, a squirrel sneaked into the garden, grabbed his bag of peanuts, and ran away.

vocabulary.chinese: 被隔離的；偏遠的　kept away from, separated from, or far from other people, animals, or places
vocabulary.example_en: The male rabbit is kept in an isolated cage to prevent the females from becoming pregnant.
Since Mary has to complete most of her tasks alone, she often feels isolated at work.
The vet isolated the puppy from the other dogs because it was sick.
The patient with the contagious disease has been kept in isolation in the hospital for a week.
Isolation from family and friends can lead to mental health problems such as depression.

vocabulary.chinese: 獲得；贏得　to receive or get something, either by working for it or asking for it
vocabulary.example_en: By asking his teacher many good questions, Sam obtained some very useful advice.

vocabulary.chinese: 說服；勸說（某人做某事）　to persuade; to talk someone into doing something
vocabulary.example_en: It’s difficult to convince Des to sell her old jeep and get a newer, safer vehicle because she loves that old car too much.
Despite Warren’s efforts to convince everyone of his honesty, nobody believed he was telling the truth.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 行李箱　a case or container, usually with wheels and in the shape of a rectangle, for carrying clothes and other travel items
vocabulary.example_en: Francis is flying to Singapore tomorrow, and she needs to pack a suitcase with some clothes and other items tonight.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 麻布（或厚紙、塑膠等）大袋　a large, loose but strong bag, usually for keeping and carrying goods like rice, flour, or sand
vocabulary.example_en: At traditional markets, you can still see beans, seeds, and grains sold in large cotton sacks.

vocabulary.chinese: 偷運；走私　to illegally take or bring someone or something into or out of an area or country
vocabulary.example_en: The criminals who tried to smuggle cigarettes into this country were caught and arrested at the border.
A sniffer dog circled the drug smuggler’s luggage twice. Then it sat down next to his suitcase and started barking.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 救護車　a small bus or van for taking injured or sick people to the hospital as quickly as possible
vocabulary.example_en: Two people were badly hurt in the accident and had to be taken to the hospital in an ambulance.

vocabulary.chinese: 勇敢的；無畏的　brave; having or showing courage despite considerable risk or danger
vocabulary.example_en: It was very courageous of Allen to save that little girl from drowning in the river.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 處決；處死　killing a criminal as punishment for a very serious crime
vocabulary.example_en: The person guilty of the murder has been sentenced to death and is currently in jail awaiting execution.
Coach Keller gave his players a clear strategy, and their brilliant execution of it enabled them to win the game.
Though these soldiers weren’t executed for belonging to the enemy army, they were locked up until the war came to an end.
Now that all the details of the mission were clear, the rescue team could jump into action and execute their plan.

vocabulary.chinese: 偽造的；假的；仿製的　made to look real but not actually true, genuine, or official
vocabulary.example_en: Anyone who tries to cross the border with a fake passport will be held until he or she can be correctly identified.
Rather than being a real da Vinci, the painting proved to be a fake and worth very little.
Faking someone’s signature on a check or a contract is a very serious crime and could even land you in jail.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 證件；文件　a formal or official piece of writing that can be used to prove something
vocabulary.example_en: Historical documents such as birth, marriage, and death records help to date all the important events in people’s lives.

vocabulary.chinese: 無私的　showing more concern for others than for oneself
vocabulary.example_en: Mother Teresa showed selfless devotion by helping the poor and those in need.

vocabulary.chinese: vi. 承認　(admitted—admitted—admitting) to agree or recognize that something is true or correct, usually without really wanting to do so
vocabulary.example_en: It takes great courage for people to admit their mistakes and apologize in time.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 勇敢　courage shown in the face of great danger or great risk
vocabulary.example_en: Ellen showed great bravery in speaking to her employer and fighting for her rights and those of her colleagues.

vocabulary.chinese: ..　收留　to let someone stay or live in your apartment or house, usually because he or she needs care or protection

grammar.explanation: 為「與過去事實相反」的假設語氣，表示實際上蓋世太保特務沒能早點進入屋內。
「然而」，用來表達語氣的轉折。前後語意相反
「因為」，用來說明原因。同 because, since
「設法做到；勉力完成」，指所做的事情是具有挑戰性或不易完成的。
「甚至；還要」，用來修飾比較級，通常置於比較級的前面。具有相同功能的程度副詞還包括：a lot、far、much、still。
「最後；終於」，表示某個過程或事件所發生的最終結果。
「從A到B」，主要用來表示某事物的「範圍」。
「某人被給予某物」，此為授予動詞的被動語態。此處是以間接受詞（通常是「人」）當作新的主詞，如果要以直接受詞（通常是「事物」）當作新的主詞，則其句型為sth. be given to sb.。

6.title: Lesson 6Dabbawalas*: Door-to-Door Meal Delivery in Mumbai*

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 組織；公司；企業　a company that aims to make money
vocabulary.example_en: Mrs. Watson’s clothing company employs over a thousand workers. It is therefore a very large enterprise.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 交易；買賣　a specific deal or sale that people complete while doing business together
vocabulary.example_en: Every time someone buys something at the convenience store, the transaction is saved in the shop’s computer system.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 比率　the number of times something happens in a certain period of time
vocabulary.example_en: The total number of visitors to the zoo is increasing at a rate of around fifty thousand people a year.

vocabulary.chinese: （用於強調不大、不重要或效果不大）僅僅的；只不過的

grammar.formula: there is/are句型通常用來表示
grammar.explanation: 「存在；有」的意思。而在比較正式或文學的語境中，「there + 動詞」也常用來描述狀態或動作，例如live, remain, follow等動詞。

grammar.formula: 此類複合形容詞結構（adv.-p.p.）由被動句型
grammar.explanation: 「be + p.p. + adv.」中的副詞結合過去分詞所構成。當副詞為well時，通常表示「備受……的」，如well-known、well-educated等。若以-ly結尾的副詞修飾過去分詞時，則不需使用連字號（-），如highly respected、deeply moved。
「而不是」，可用來描述被取代的事物或未做的動作。
「儘管如此；即使那樣」，意同nevertheless, nonetheless, still。
「擔任（某種身分、職務或角色）」，後接名詞。
「不管；無論」，後面接名詞或名詞片語，說明主要子句所描述的情況不受其後內容的影響。
意為「出於；因為」，用來表示行為的動機、原因或出發點。
意為「一……就……」。可與As soon as S + V..., S + V....代換。Upon/On之後須接動名詞或名詞，As soon as之後須接子句。
意為「必定……」，用來表示對過去事實的肯定推測。本段後續亦採用相同用法，故不再另行標註。
